response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with a long and credible track record, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. He shows no Japanese language ability (a hard requirement at native level), only brief C/C++ exposure from a six-month stint in 2014-2015 rather than strong C-for-UNIX proficiency, and no evidence of Linux kernel work, L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. The gaps span nearly every required qualification, so an interview would not be a good use of either side's time.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some systems-level work
Some low-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services handling high request volumes
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching/workshops
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, which is a non-negotiable native-level requirement
C/UNIX experience is minimal and dated; no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience
No background in L2/L3 networking, LAN switches, or security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, authentication, tamper detection)

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
